LIVE TEST (user, v0.4.1 RTX PRO 6000 Pod): cu128 image booted clean (REMOTE ONLINE 96GB VRAM/283GB RAM) — image build VERIFIED good. BUT MPI-95 install progress made it WORSE: still snapped to ~80% on press + the new Verifying sweep was less helpful than the old determinate tail. Root cause = APP bug, NOT wrapper, NO rebuild: modelJob.totalBytes (denominator) was seeded once from rounded registry sizes and never re-derived from the wrapper _resolve_total per-dep real totals → real bytes outran rounded denominator → snap. Fix (app commit dc27f33, RunPod, unpushed): _onRemoteInstallEvent re-derives modelJob.totalBytes from per-dep totals every tick (kills snap) + install-verifying now re-broadcasts a DETERMINATE tick so the bar holds at real % (no sweep). Wrapper still emits the events (kept as a seam). Re-test = install a model on the SAME Pod (no redeploy). cache-lru/503 tests still pending. Card stays doing.

note: CANDIDATE #6 CODE WRITTEN + LOCALLY VALIDATED (awaiting v0.4.2 build). Wrapper now OWNS+supervises ComfyUI (ComfyManager) + POST /wrapper/restart-comfy → restarts ONLY ComfyUI on a per-model node install (no Pod reboot, no local detour, no error+GitHub dialog). Live-tested the RunPod ops first: console Restart=NO-OP, Reset=wipes container, Stop→Start=only thing that reloads ComfyUI but full GPU reboot + dropped app to LOCAL while Pod billed → hence the proper wrapper endpoint. ARCH: start.sh execs the wrapper (now container main proc); wrapper spawns ComfyUI with byte-identical args (incl --cache-lru 2); unexpected ComfyUI death → wrapper os._exit → container down (old wait -n safety preserved); inert in download mode (-cpu). App (comfyController.js:300): error dialog → /proxy/restart-comfy + toast + poll ready + auto-retry; 404 fallback to manual msg for old image. Commits: mpi-ci 9b0a07e (pushed), app remoteProxy.js+comfyController.js (RunPod, pending commit). Bump v0.4.1→v0.4.2 / 0.2.4→0.2.5. Local validation: py_compile/bash -n/node -c/eslint clean + wrapper smoke-boot (health up, restart token-guarded+409 download-mode, arg list identical). Also logged #7: app desync bug (running+billing Pod shows LOCAL·OFFLINE, app-only no rebuild). UNTESTABLE locally = real subprocess spawn/restart on live Pod → USER verifies after build.

VERIFIED LIVE on L4 Pod: project-page slot flips last session -> current session, renders climbing duration/cost (30s/$0.00 -> 1min/$0.01), matches RunPod console Uptime+cost. App commit 1d39aa9 (heroStats render+format+label, shell.js per-tick specs re-emit, index.html label id). Server specs payload (lastStartedAt-uptime + costPerHr) landed earlier in HEAD. Bugs found+fixed: runtime.uptimeInSeconds is GraphQL-only (null on REST) -> use lastStartedAt+costPerHr; feed was edge-only -> per-tick re-fetch; added seconds to format ladder.

BUG #8 FIXED (app-only, commit 1c6b800, no rebuild): remote uninstall deleted SHARED deps = the GC bug user flagged. Root cause: _findOtherModelsUsingDep filters MODELS[].installed===true but that flag is renderer-only (syncModelInstalled), ALWAYS undefined in the backend Node process → guard returned 0 → kept 0 shared → uninstalling Wan I2V trashed wan_2.1_vae + umt5 text-encoder that Wan T2V shares → T2V went PARTIAL/NOT-INSTALLED. Fix: new _remoteSharedDepIds resolves shared deps from the actual Pod VOLUME via remoteModelsCheck (wrapper), keeps any dep an installed-on-volume model still needs; fails SAFE (aborts uninstall if volume check fails). Local path unchanged (renderer flag valid there). node -c+eslint clean. BUG #9 NOTED (wrapper-side, NOT yet fixed): a collided/cancelled remote install leaves orphan .part files (seen live: umt5 + i2v-high/low stuck .part after a T2V+I2V install collision); models/status can mis-read a .part as present/absent → UI desync (install no-op, wrong NOT-INSTALLED/PARTIAL label). Ties to [[project_ndh_resumable_downloads]] models/check false-positive-on-partials. Recovery = rm the .part files + refresh. Needs a wrapper decision (clean .part on abort + status ignores .part) — candidate for a FUTURE build, do NOT pull into v0.4.2. Also #2 same UX family: install collision shows Download-Failed+GitHub dialog (should be a toast).

App restart CLEARED the stuck state — T2V now correctly shows PARTIALLY INSTALLED (30.3/36.5GB). Confirms the earlier NOT-INSTALLED label + Install-no-op were STUCK _modelJobs session state (from tonight collided installs), NOT code bugs — same class as the PonyMix no-op, cleared by restart. BACKLOG #10 (app-side, NOT v0.4.2, user-requested): concurrent installs must work — installing T2V + I2V at the same time collides on a shared dep (umt5) and the second is REJECTED whole (this model is already downloading). The guard is too coarse: a second model that needs an in-flight dep should SHARE/await it (refcount the dep, attach the new model to the running download) instead of failing. Pairs with #2 (collision shows Download-Failed+GitHub dialog → should be toast/silent). User wants: install every model at once. Deferred — finishing the v0.4.2 restart-feature live test first.

note: SESSION PAUSE — restart-feature live-test PARTLY done, not fully closed. STATE: v0.4.2-cu124 built+pushed+public; boot VERIFIED on L4 (wrapper 0.2.5, comfy_ready, ComfyUI spawned BY the wrapper with byte-identical args incl --cache-lru 2 — the risky wrapper-owns-ComfyUI restructure WORKS). NOT yet verified: the actual install-I2V→gen-I2V→auto-restart-toast flow (ran out of time installing the models). T2V + I2V now BOTH installed on the volume (clean). NEXT SESSION: restart app (loads committed fixes), reconnect L4-or-new v0.4.2 Pod, gen I2V → confirm toast Loading-new-nodes + NO error/GitHub dialog + gen runs. THEN build cu128 (local) + cpu (CI) v0.4.2. App commits on RunPod (unpushed): dea1681 (auto-restart), 1c6b800 (GC shared-dep fix). mpi-ci 9b0a07e pushed. NEW BUG found this session = SSE-DROP HANG: the remote install event stream (remote install SSE closed / SSE stream aborted: terminated) drops mid-install → app never gets install-complete → bar sits at 100% for minutes even though the Pod finished the file (confirmed via ls = no .part). NOT the MPI-95 progress fix (% math unchanged) — a transport drop. FIX (app-side, MPI-97 family): poll /wrapper/models/install/active as fallback when the SSE drops, instead of hanging. Reproduced on BOTH T2V (umt5) + I2V (big i2v files, worse). USER must Stop/Delete the running L4 Pod (volume persists).

RESTART-FEATURE TEST NOT YET VALID — first gen attempt did NOT exercise the restart path. Why: app auto-connect-on-start created a FRESH Pod, so ComfyUI booted AFTER the Painter node was already on the volume → it scanned the node at boot → comfyNeedsRestart was NOT set → the gen just worked (node already loaded), restart path never fired. To actually test, the node must install onto an ALREADY-RUNNING ComfyUI. EXACT REPRO for next session: (1) Connect (fresh Pod, both video nodes load clean at boot); (2) Uninstall I2V (GC fix 1c6b800 now keeps shared umt5/vae, removes only i2v weights + Painter node); (3) Gen T2V → confirms engine live; (4) Reinstall I2V → Painter lands on the LIVE ComfyUI → comfyNeedsRestart set; (5) Gen I2V → EXPECT toast Loading-new-nodes-restarting + auto ComfyUI restart (~15s) + gen runs, with NO Generation-failed/Report-on-GitHub dialog + NO Settings trip. Watch logs/app.log for /proxy/restart-comfy. Feature is BUILT (v0.4.2-cu124 public) + boot-verified + code-validated; only this path unconfirmed (low risk). After it passes: build cu128 (local Docker) + cpu (CI) for v0.4.2. ALT repro the user noted: disable auto-connect-on-start so ComfyUI is already up before the node install.

G2 REVISIT (user feedback 2026-06-15): the Stopping toast (Stopping… the remote engine is interrupting the current step) is UNWANTED on a USER-initiated stop — stopping a generation should NOT trigger a toast at all. G2 originally specced ADDING a Stopping toast for the ~5s Stop->interrupt gap; user now says it is noise. DECISION NEEDED: either drop the toast on user-initiated stop entirely, or make it much quieter/shorter. App-side, no rebuild. Surfaced while live-testing the MPI-81 remote ComfyUI restart (user start+stopped a T2V gen to confirm ComfyUI was running before installing I2V).

✅ RESTART FEATURE VERIFIED LIVE (candidate #6). Valid repro finally run: ComfyUI confirmed running (T2V start+stop), Painter node rm-d from volume, app restart → ComfyUI booted WITHOUT Painter, installed I2V (Painter lands on the LIVE ComfyUI → comfyNeedsRestart set), gen I2V → the app showed the toast "Loading new nodes — restarting the remote engine…" (the exact string from comfyController.js auto-restart branch) with NO Generation-failed/Report-on-GitHub dialog + NO Settings trip. The /proxy/restart-comfy success path is silent in logs/app.log (logs only on error) so the on-screen toast is the proof — it only renders when the restart branch executes; old code would have thrown the error dialog. v0.4.2-cu124 wrapper-owns-ComfyUI + /wrapper/restart-comfy WORKS end to end. REMAINING: confirm the post-restart I2V gen produces a video (two Generation-completed-but-no-output warnings seen — may be the interrupted gen settling or the L4 RAM wall on I2V, SEPARATE from the restart feature which already passed). THEN build cu128 (local Docker) + cpu (CI) for v0.4.2 + push. App commits unpushed on RunPod: dea1681 (auto-restart) + 1c6b800 (GC fix). mpi-ci 9b0a07e pushed.
